perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall have the non-exclusive right and license to develop and distribute ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products during the Term of the Agreement for all Wireless Products set forth in this Paragraph 2(c) except the Game, for which Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute until December 31, 2006.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products during the Term of the Agreement for all Wireless Products set forth in this Paragraph 2(c), including the Game, for which Licensee shall have the exclusive right and license to develop and distribute until December 31, 2007.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Licensee has non-exclusive rights to all ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products except the Game, for which they have exclusive rights until December 31, 2006. The modification changes this to grant Licensee exclusive rights to all ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products, including the Game, until December 31, 2007. This contradicts the later clause that provides Fox reserved rights to distribute games after January 1, 2007, creating an In-Text Contradiction on exclusivity period of the distribution of games.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (A) "ICE AGE 2" RESERVATION OF RIGHTS: Fox hereby reserves the right to develop, publish and/or distribute (or grant a third party the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute) any wireless products or wireless content or any nature which may be derived from the property "ICE AGE 2". Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no event will Fox develop, publish and/or distribute games derived from the Property "ICE AGE 2" prior to January 1, 2007.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants the Licensee exclusive rights to all ICE AGE 2 Wireless Products, including the Game, until December 31, 2007. This directly contradicts the 'ICE AGE 2' RESERVATION OF RIGHTS, which states that Fox reserves the right to develop, publish, and/or distribute games derived from the Property 'ICE AGE 2' after January 1, 2007. This creates a clear conflict regarding the exclusivity period for game distribution, satisfying the condition of Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction because the "exclusivity" is inconsistently defined in different parts of the contract. The change gives Licensee exclusivity until the end of 2007, whilst Fox holds the right to distribute after January 1, 2007. This would cause a clear in-text contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Should Fox or its assigns choose to distribute the Wireless Products through Fox's and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, Fox or its assigns shall deduct a percentage of Fox's Gross Receipts (defined as monies received by or credited to Fox or its assigns from Fox's or its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, for the download of the Wireless Products by end users, or the sale or download of Wireless Products to end users) collected from the sale of such Wireless Products ("Distribution Fee") in the amount of ***** percent (*****%) of Fox's Gross Receipts.
perturbation.changed_text: Should Fox or its assigns choose to distribute the Wireless Products through Fox's and its assigns' direct-to-consumer distribution channels, Fox or its assigns shall deduct a Distribution Fee (defined as a fee calculated at Fox's discretion) from the gross revenues. This Distribution Fee can be adjusted at any time by Fox without prior notice to the Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the Distribution Fee as a percentage of Fox's Gross Receipts. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the fee is 'calculated at Fox's discretion' and can be 'adjusted at any time', contradicting the later requirement to remit royalties 'in the amounts set forth in the Agreement'. This creates uncertainty about the actual royalty amounts, due to the contradiction on what entails Fox's Gross Receipts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: After Fox or its assigns deducts its Distribution Fee, it shall remit the remainder to Licensee and Licensee shall pay to Fox or its assigns or such other party as Fox or its assigns may designate in writing, Royalties in the amounts set forth in the Agreement or this Amendment.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the Distribution Fee as a percentage of Gross Receipts. The changed text, however, defines it as a fee 'calculated at Fox's discretion' and adjustable at any time. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that royalties are remitted 'in the amounts set forth in the Agreement'. Because the fee can now be arbitrarily changed, the 'amounts set forth in the Agreement' become uncertain and unenforceable, resulting in an in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities definition.
